rulings.Case Title: CONCORDE EQUITY 11, LLC VS. Q-BLACK, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Order: Instructing Receiver As To The Distribution Of Proceeds From The Sale Of Liquior License
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 31, 2011, line 1. KEVIN SINGER'S Motion For Order: Instructing Receiver As To The Distribution Of Proceeds From The Sale Of Liquior License. Grant. Money to be disbursed to the Receiver and Concorde Equity II only. B & P 24076 inapplicable. Debtor agreed to general security interest that was not specific to liquor license. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MATEEL ENVIRONMENTAL JUSTICE FOUNDATION VS. APACHE HOSE & BELTING COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Prop 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment;

rulings.Case Title: AMY CHAMBERLIN VS. ROSS STORES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Approval Of Class Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 31, 2011, line 4. PLAINTIFF AMY CHAMBERLIN'S Motion For Final Approval Of Class Settlement. Continued to February 7, 2011. Moving party to provide courtesy copies of its papers to department 302 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. San Francisco Local Rule 2.6 B. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MAX LIMITED LLC, VS. ELIZABETH FALKNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 31, 2011, line 5. PLAINTIFF MAX LIMITED L L C'S Motion For Leave To File A First Amended Complaint. Grant, conditioned on plaintiff agreeing to a 90 day trial continuance. If plaintiff does not agree, then the motion is denied without prejudice. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE D. CHAMBERLIN VS. AVEDA EXPERIENCE CENTER INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Final Approval Of Class Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 31, 2011, line 11. Plaintiff's Motion For Final Approval Of Class Settlement. Hearing required. Attorneys to provide detailed time records showing dates and times of hours expended. Declaration of Robert B. Hansen inadequate. Attorneys to be prepared to explain why it was necessary to have 2 counsel work on case. Plaintiff and defendant to provide number of individuals since the ad was placed who have requested or indicate that they will request cash refunds. Plaintiff and defendant to provide a statement of what training was engaged in for store employees. =(302/AJR)
